seo.meta_description: Import an Audi A6 Avant from Japan to NZ. See model details, auction process, cost components and what to confirm before bidding.
seo.og_title: Audi A6 Avant import NZ | ImportYourCar.co.nz
seo.og_description: Looking at a Japanese-market Audi A6 Avant for New Zealand? We explain the model details, auction information, landed cost components and import process.
secondary_keywords: import Audi A6 Avant New Zealand
Audi A6 Avant auction Japan NZ

blocks.hero_subtitle: The A6 Avant works well if you want a premium wagon with real luggage space and Audi’s quattro drivetrain. We can check live Japanese auction availability, explain the listing information and help you decide whether the exact car suits your brief.
proof_points: Importing Estimate, compliance is part of the process
blocks.why_kiwis_buy: Some buyers want the space of a wagon without moving into an SUV, and that is where the A6 Avant makes sense. Audi Japan lists 466L of luggage space for this model, which is a useful detail because it shows the boot is genuinely usable rather than just stylish on paper. It also suits buyers chasing executive comfort, a refined cabin and quiet long-distance driving in a wagon body.

savings_block.headline: Where the money goes, from Japan purchase to NZ landed cost
savings_block.body: We’ll provide you with an import estimate with the cost of the car, expenses in Japan, our fee, customs clearance, compliance and on-road costs, plus any other expenses that may vary depending on the vehicle. This will give you a clear ballpark figure, so you’ll have a pretty good idea of the total cost upfront.

cta_form.headline: Find your A6 Avant, we'll do the searching
cta_form.subtext: Fill in your details to get started. We'll search the Japanese auctions and come back with real options and pricing by email.

blocks.video_caption: If the video shows the rear area, it is worth noting the wagon body properly. Audi Japan lists 466L of luggage capacity, so this shape is doing a real job, not just changing the look of the car.
blocks.main_usp_deep: The 2020 A6 Avant TFSI quattro 200kW combines a 2.0-litre turbo-petrol four-cylinder with 200kW and 400Nm, a 7-speed S tronic gearbox and quattro all-wheel drive. That means strong mid-range pull for overtaking and motorway merges, without the car feeling strained when it is loaded with passengers or luggage. It is the right sort of setup for a premium wagon that will spend time on New Zealand open roads as well as around town.

buyer_persona.headline: Who is this car right for?
buyer_persona.body: This suits someone who wants a premium wagon for business use, family travel or open-road work, and who values cabin refinement as much as luggage space. It also suits buyers who want executive comfort, a high-spec interior feel and composed motorway driving, while understanding that wheel, brake and electronic-option costs sit above mainstream wagons. If that lines up with how you use a car, this is a solid match.

trust_company.headline: Why import with ImportYourCar.co.nz?
trust_company.body: We have been helping New Zealand buyers import from Japan since 2009. Our role is to search the auction market, explain the information available for each car, provide pricing guidance and handle the purchase and shipping process once you approve a bid. We also support the NZ arrival and compliance steps, while being clear about what can and cannot be confirmed before the car is here.

car_specs_benefits.benefit: A smaller-capacity turbo engine suits this kind of wagon well, giving useful pulling power without needing a large-displacement engine.
That output gives the A6 Avant the sort of performance that makes overtaking and motorway merging feel easy.
Torque matters in a wagon like this, because it is what gives the car relaxed mid-range response with passengers or luggage on board.
Audi’s dual-clutch transmission gives quick ratio changes and helps the car feel more direct than a conventional automatic.
That is proper wagon space for cases, work gear or a weekly load of shopping without needing to fold seats straight away.

standout_features_block.headline: What sets it apart
standout_features_block.intro: These are the practical features that make the A6 Avant more than a stylish alternative to an SUV. Equipment varies by car, so we confirm each one on the auction listing.

items.title: 200kW turbo engine and quattro drive
items.body: This version pairs 200kW and 400Nm with quattro all-wheel drive, so it has the mid-range shove needed for overtaking and the sure-footed feel many Kiwi drivers want in wet weather. We confirm the exact engine and drivetrain on the individual auction listing.
items.image_hint: Audi A6 Avant on a wet motorway on-ramp, front three-quarter angle showing composed stance

items.title: Matrix LED headlights
items.body: Matrix LED headlights use individually controlled LEDs to shape the high beam around other traffic. They are worth looking for if you do regular night driving on open roads, but they are an option, so we check the listing and photos rather than assume they are fitted.
items.image_hint: Close-up night shot of the headlight signature illuminated on the front corner of the car

items.title: Bang & Olufsen 3D sound
items.body: The optional Bang & Olufsen system adds a higher-grade speaker setup and 3D processing. It is a genuine everyday upgrade for drivers who spend a lot of time in the car, but it is not standard on every A6 Avant, so we confirm it from the listing and cabin photos.
items.image_hint: Interior shot focused on speaker grille detail in the front door and dashboard area

items.title: 466L boot in a proper wagon body
items.body: With 466L of boot space, the A6 Avant has room for work gear, airport bags or the weekly shop without moving to an SUV. You get useful carrying space while keeping the lower, more car-like driving position and road manners of a wagon.
items.image_hint: Rear tailgate open with suitcases or work bags loaded into the boot

tech_deep_dive_block.headline: The technology, explained
tech_deep_dive_block.intro: These are the main systems worth understanding before you shortlist one.

items.title: 2.0 TFSI 200kW powertrain
items.body: The 2.0-litre turbo petrol engine produces 200kW and 400Nm in the TFSI quattro version. The 400Nm matters on New Zealand roads: it gives the wagon strong response for passing traffic and joining the motorway, even with people and luggage on board.

items.title: 7-speed S tronic
items.body: Audi Japan states a 7-speed S tronic transmission for this version. S tronic is Audi’s dual-clutch gearbox, so it shifts through pre-selected ratios rather than using a conventional torque-converter layout. Behind the wheel, that means a more direct feel when you accelerate and cleaner response when the road opens up.

items.title: quattro all-wheel drive
items.body: The Japanese-market A6 Avant featured here is the quattro all-wheel-drive model. It gives the car assured traction and a composed feel on long journeys, making it a strong option for buyers who prefer an all-wheel-drive wagon.

items.title: Matrix LED and audio options
items.body: Matrix LED headlights and Bang & Olufsen 3D sound are available on Japanese-market A6 Avant models. We confirm the equipment fitted to each auction car from its listing and photos, so you know exactly what you are buying.

cta_mid_page.headline: Want us to search current A6 Avant auction cars?
cta_mid_page.subtext: Send us your year, budget and must-have features, and we will come back with real options.

models_comparison.intro: We compare the exact auction listings available at the time of your search, rather than relying on generic variant assumptions.

rows.notes: Checked for the exact model code and vehicle before bidding, final certification occurs in NZ.
The 2020 A6 Avant TFSI quattro 200kW uses a 1,984cc turbo petrol engine with 200kW, 400Nm and a 7-speed S tronic gearbox. Confirm the exact engine on the individual auction listing.
Features such as Matrix LED headlights and Bang & Olufsen 3D sound appear on some Japanese-market specifications. Exact fitment must be confirmed on the individual car.
Current availability needs to be checked against live Japanese auction data.

steps.title: Tell us what you're after
steps.body: Fill in the form or message us. We confirm your brief and give you a cost breakdown before anything moves forward.

steps.title: We search for relevant auction cars
steps.body: We can send current options and explain the listing information available for each car, including grade, mileage, recorded condition notes and listed modifications where supplied.

steps.title: You approve, we bid
steps.body: We send you the auction sheet and our recommendation. Once you're happy, you pay a deposit and we bid on your behalf.

steps.title: Shipping to New Zealand
steps.body: After purchase, we coordinate shipping and keep you updated as schedules and arrival information are confirmed.

steps.title: Compliance and delivery
steps.body: The car goes through NZ entry certification and compliance. Once it's through, we arrange delivery or you can collect.

buyer_challenges_solutions.intro: Importing is easier to manage when the exact car is reviewed carefully before bidding.

items.risk: Being disappointed by condition versus expectation
items.solution: We translate and explain the auction information supplied for the car, including grade, mileage, damage markings, condition notes and listed modifications, so you can decide with the available evidence in front of you.
Before you approve a bid, we break the estimate into the main cost components so you can see how the landed figure is built.
We explain what the auction grade records, what the notes say and what the supplied photos do and do not prove.

blocks.how_to_use_iyc: You tell us the model, year range, budget and any must-have features. We can check live auction availability, explain the information supplied for each shortlisted car and give you pricing guidance before any bid is placed. If you want to see what is out there now, send us your brief and we will start with current options.

faq_block.q: Can you find a 2020 Audi A6 Avant from Japan?
faq_block.a: We can search current Japanese auction listings for you. Exact stock changes constantly, so the right starting point is your budget, year range and must-have features.

faq_block.q: What makes up the landed cost?
faq_block.a: We price the specific auction vehicle before you bid. The estimate is built from the vehicle price, Japan-side costs, shipping, service fee, customs clearance, compliance and on-road costs. Compliance and on-road costs are confirmed individually for the selected car.

faq_block.q: Can you tell me if it will get through compliance?
faq_block.a: Import viability needs to be checked for the exact model code and vehicle before bidding. Final entry certification and compliance happen in New Zealand after arrival.

faq_block.q: How long does the process take?
faq_block.a: Timing depends on when the right car appears, the shipping schedule and the NZ arrival process. We keep you updated as each stage is confirmed.

faq_block.q: How do I know what condition the auction car is in?
faq_block.a: We use the auction information supplied for that car, including grade, mileage, damage markings, notes and photos where available. That helps you make an informed decision while staying clear about what the listing does not prove.

footer_cta.headline: Send us your A6 Avant brief
footer_cta.subtext: If you want current auction options and a clear cost outline, send us the year, budget and features you want. We will come back with the available details and let you decide from there.

subscription_block.headline: Stay in the loop
subscription_block.subtext: Get auction alerts, stock updates and practical import guides by email.

blocks.company_positioning: ImportYourCar.co.nz is a New Zealand-based vehicle import service operated by Ultimate Motors Ltd. Since 2009, we have helped buyers source vehicles from Japanese auctions, understand the available listing information and coordinate the process through purchase, shipping and NZ arrival.

city_use_case.headline: How this wagon can fit NZ use
city_use_case.body: The A6 Avant can suit different parts of New Zealand depending on how you use it. The key is confirming the exact engine, wheel package, mileage and listed options on each auction car before bidding.
items: Auckland, useful for buyers wanting a European wagon for motorway and family use.
Christchurch, the wagon body and quattro layout may appeal for open-road driving.
Wellington, specification can vary by car, so check the exact engine and listed options carefully.
Hamilton or Tauranga, useful if you want a premium wagon rather than an SUV, with landed-cost guidance based on the exact listing.

landed_cost_breakdown.intro: We prepare the landed-cost estimate for the selected vehicle before you bid.

rows.notes: The agreed purchase price for the specific vehicle.
The relevant costs in Japan for that vehicle and purchase.
Freight for the confirmed vehicle and sailing.
Our service fee for arranging the import.
Clearance costs for the vehicle on arrival.
Confirmed after the vehicle and its requirements are known.
Confirmed individually for the vehicle before you proceed.

auction_information.rule: State only what the exact listing records.
Translate and explain only notes actually present.
Availability varies, never imply a complete photo or service record set.

not_for_you_block.headline: Who this may not suit
items: If you want the lowest running costs in the segment, a premium German wagon on larger wheels is probably not the right place to start.
If you need certainty on exact equipment before you begin searching, keep in mind that Japanese-market A6 Avant specification varies by car and needs to be confirmed listing by listing.

alternatives_block.intro: If the A6 Avant is close but not quite right, these are the sensible alternatives from the same Japan or NZ context.

rows.better_if: You want another German wagon sold in Japan and New Zealand.
rows.tradeoff: Exact engine and option mix also vary by car.

rows.better_if: You want a premium wagon with a similar brief around luggage space and long-distance use.
rows.tradeoff: Auction availability and equipment spread can vary a lot between listings.

rows.better_if: You want a smaller AWD wagon with broader parts familiarity.
rows.tradeoff: It does not offer the same cabin size or prestige feel as the A6 Avant.

engine_drivetrain_block.headline: Engine and drivetrain: what to check
engine_drivetrain_block.body: The 2020 A6 Avant TFSI quattro uses a 2.0-litre turbo-petrol engine with 200kW and 400Nm, a 7-speed S tronic gearbox and quattro all-wheel drive. It is a relaxed open-road wagon with useful overtaking response, but the exact engine, drivetrain and condition must still be confirmed on the individual auction listing before bidding.
checklist: Confirm the 2.0 TFSI engine, 7-speed S tronic gearbox and quattro drivetrain on the auction listing.
Check the available photos and condition notes for wheel damage, warning lights, oil leaks or aftermarket modifications.
Set out what the listing confirms and what still needs to be allowed for after the car arrives in New Zealand.
